meaning: n.(不满的职工或敌特等的)阴谋破坏,怠工,破坏vi.从事破坏活动vt.对...采取破坏行动,妨害,破坏
examples: Protesters failed to sabotage the peace talks.
Police investigating the train derailment have not ruled out sabotage.
On the contrary, many of these crimes, such as embezzlement of funds, the alteration of records, theft, vandalism, sabotage, and terrorism, can be committed without a computer.
There are a host of issues that can sabotage your plans in times like these.
Right-wing French terrorists reportedly tried to return the favor by attempting to sabotage Mattei's plane.
